sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页主要讲 Overview：Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that reads your codebase, edits files, runs commands, and integrates with your development tools. Available in your terminal, IDE, desktop app, and browser.
你可以把它当成“Getting started”这块里专门管这一摊事的说明书。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Overview”理解成 Getting started 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
原文这页大多会按 Get started、What you can do、Use Claude Code everywhere、Next steps 这些环节往下讲。
翻成人话，大概就是：Use Claude Code everywhere

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：Get started -> What you can do -> Use Claude Code everywhere -> Next steps。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Get started 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Choose your environment to get started. Most surfaces require a Claude subscription or Anthropic Console account. The Terminal CLI and VS Code also support third-party providers. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：What you can do 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Here are some of the ways you can use Claude Code: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：What you can do 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Here are some of the ways you can use Claude Code: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: claude "commit my changes with a descriptive message"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：What you can do 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Here are some of the ways you can use Claude Code: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Monitor logs and get alerted
tail -f app.log | claude -p "Slack me if you see any anomalies"

# Automate translations in CI
claude -p "translate new strings into French and raise a PR for review"

# Bulk operations across files
git diff main --name-only | claude -p "review these changed files for security issues"

你可以把它当成“Getting started”这块里专门管这一摊事的说明书。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Changelog”理解成 Getting started 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
翻成人话，大概就是：Release notes for Claude Code, including new features, improvements, and bug fixes by version.

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：2.1.77 -> 2.1.76 -> 2.1.75 -> 2.1.74。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Claude Code 到底是按什么路数干活的。
不是瞎猜瞎改，而是先看、再想、再动手、再回头验。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把它想成一个肯先绕地一圈的老把式。它不是看见一处泥巴就乱铲，而是先把地形、水路、工具和活路摸清。
这一页最值钱的是把“代理循环”“模型”“工具”“能碰到什么材料”这些底层门道讲明白。
你越懂这页，后面就越知道什么时候让它先分析，什么时候再让它真改。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 遇到大活，先让它摸底和列计划，不要一上来就吩咐大改。
知道它能看什么、能动什么、能跑什么命令，后面提要求才不容易超边界。
如果它跑偏，多半不是它突然变笨，而是你交代得太散、太大、太混。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Resume or fork sessions
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：When you resume a session with claude --continue or claude --resume, you pick up where you left off using the same session ID. New messages append to the existing conversation. Your full conversation history is restored, but session-scoped permissions are not. You’ll need to re-approve those. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Claude Code 身上都能再挂哪些家伙事。
不是只会一个人单打独斗，它还能接规矩、接技能、接外部工具、接自动化。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像给拖拉机看挂载清单。原机能开，但挂上不同设备以后，能耕地、能播种、能拉货。
这一页最重要的是帮你分清：什么时候该用 `CLAUDE.md`，什么时候该上 skills、subagents、hooks、MCP、plugins。
它不是叫你全装，而是叫你别装错。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从眼前最缺的一项能力补，不要为了“功能全”一口气全挂上。
几个功能名字看着像，实际负责的事情不一样，这页就是拿来分清边界的。
加功能之前先想收益，再想复杂度，别把帮工越改越笨重。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Core concepts 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页主要讲 Store instructions and memories：Give Claude persistent instructions with CLAUDE.md files, and let Claude accumulate learnings automatically with auto memory.
你可以把它当成“Core concepts”这块里专门管这一摊事的说明书。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把“Store instructions and memories”理解成 Core concepts 这一栏里的一把专门工具。这页不是让你背书，而是教你什么时候该把这把工具拿出来。
原文这页大多会按 CLAUDE.md vs auto memory、CLAUDE.md files、Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files、Set up a project CLAUDE.md 这些环节往下讲。
翻成人话，大概就是：CLAUDE.md vs auto memory

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一，先别一上来全开全配。先按最小一步试通，确认没跑偏，再继续往下加。
第二，命令、配置名、参数名这些硬东西尽量保留原样。人话解释是帮你听懂，不是帮你改关键字。
第三，照着原文这几个环节挨个过：CLAUDE.md vs auto memory -> CLAUDE.md files -> Choose where to put CLAUDE.md files -> Set up a project CLAUDE.md。像下地先看水路、再试机器、再正式开干，一步一步最稳。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Import additional files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
See @README for project overview and @package.json for available npm commands for this project.

# Additional Instructions
- git workflow @docs/git-instructions.md

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Import additional files 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：CLAUDE.md files can import additional files using @path/to/import syntax. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：How CLAUDE.md files load
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md files by walking up the directory tree from your current working directory, checking each directory along the way. This means if you run Claude Code in foo/bar/, it loads instructions from both foo/bar/CLAUDE.md and foo/CLAUDE.md. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
your-project/
├── .claude/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md           # Main project instructions
│   └── rules/
│       ├── code-style.md   # Code style guidelines
│       ├── testing.md      # Testing conventions
│       └── security.md     # Security requirements

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
paths:
  - "src/api/**/*.ts"
---

# API Development Rules

- All API endpoints must include input validation
- Use the standard error response format
- Include OpenAPI documentation comments

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Organize rules with .claude/rules/ 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger projects, you can organize instructions into multiple files using the .claude/rules/ directory. This keeps instructions modular and easier for teams to maintain. Rules can also be scoped to specific file paths, so they only load into context when Claude works with matching files, reducing noise and saving context space. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Manage CLAUDE.md for large teams
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：For organizations deploying Claude Code across teams, you can centralize instructions and control which CLAUDE.md files are loaded. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Enable or disable auto memory
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Auto memory is on by default. To toggle it, open /memory in a session and use the auto memory toggle, or set autoMemoryEnabled in your project settings: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Storage location 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Storage location 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository, so all worktrees and subdirectories within the same repo share one auto memory directory. Outside a git repo, the project root is used instead. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/
├── MEMORY.md          # Concise index, loaded into every session
├── debugging.md       # Detailed notes on debugging patterns
├── api-conventions.md # API design decisions
└── ...                # Any other topic files Claude creates

lines: 这是 Core concepts 里的一摊要紧活

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲日常最常见的派活套路。
像看新项目、找相关代码、修 bug、重构、补测试，这些都给了顺手走法。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像老农教你干活的顺序：先看地，再找水，再下种，不是抡起锄头就全翻。
这一页特别适合新手，因为它不是讲抽象能力，而是把常见活拆成一步一步。
你照着这些套路派活，比自己临场乱说稳得多。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 新项目先看全貌，再找相关文件，不要一开始就陷进一个函数里。
修 bug 前先定位范围，再动手，再验结果，这个节奏别乱。
如果活很大，就拆成几段：摸底、实现、验证，各段分开看。

sections.title: Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。
这里还牵扯作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

steps.title: Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：When working on multiple tasks at once, you need each Claude session to have its own copy of the codebase so changes don’t collide. Git worktrees solve this by creating separate working directories that each have their own files and branch, while sharing the same repository history and remote connections. This means you can have Claude working on a feature in one worktree while fixing a bug in another, without either session interfering with the other. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Start Claude in a worktree named "feature-auth"
# Creates .claude/worktrees/feature-auth/ with a new branch
claude --worktree feature-auth

# Start another session in a separate worktree
claude --worktree bugfix-123

steps.title: Run parallel Claude Code sessions with Git worktrees 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：When working on multiple tasks at once, you need each Claude session to have its own copy of the codebase so changes don’t collide. Git worktrees solve this by creating separate working directories that each have their own files and branch, while sharing the same repository history and remote connections. This means you can have Claude working on a feature in one worktree while fixing a bug in another, without either session interfering with the other. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

这里还牵扯作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。

steps.title: Manage worktrees manually
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：For more control over worktree location and branch configuration, create worktrees with Git directly. This is useful when you need to check out a specific existing branch or place the worktree outside the repository. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Create a worktree with a new branch
git worktree add ../project-feature-a -b feature-a

# Create a worktree with an existing branch
git worktree add ../project-bugfix bugfix-123

# Start Claude in the worktree
cd ../project-feature-a && claude

# Clean up when done
git worktree list
git worktree remove ../project-feature-a

steps.title: Get notified when Claude needs your attention 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：When you kick off a long-running task and switch to another window, you can set up desktop notifications so you know when Claude finishes or needs your input. This uses the Notification hook event, which fires whenever Claude is waiting for permission, idle and ready for a new prompt, or completing authentication. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Core concepts 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么把 Claude Code 使唤得更顺，不是讲新功能，是讲怎么少返工。
核心就一句话：话说清、边界画清、验收讲清。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 再好的帮工，也怕遇到一句“你帮我弄一下”的糊涂单。
这一页会教你怎么给它验证办法、怎么先探索再规划、怎么提供更具体的上下文和更有用的材料。
它不是花招，而是你以后少踩坑的基本功。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 别只说目标，也要说约束和验收方式。
给它丰富上下文时，宁可给关键材料，也别一股脑塞一车废话。
能让它自己验证的事，尽量给它验证条件，这比让它蒙着干靠谱得多。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Write an effective CLAUDE.md 1
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Run /init to generate a starter CLAUDE.md file based on your current project structure, then refine over time. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Code style
- Use ES modules (import/export) syntax, not CommonJS (require)
- Destructure imports when possible (eg. import { foo } from 'bar')

# Workflow
- Be sure to typecheck when you're done making a series of code changes
- Prefer running single tests, and not the whole test suite, for performance

# Additional Instructions

- Personal overrides: @~/.claude/my-project-instructions.md

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create skills 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Create SKILL.md files in .claude/skills/ to give Claude domain knowledge and reusable workflows. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: api-conventions
description: REST API design conventions for our services
---
# API Conventions
- Use kebab-case for URL paths
- Use camelCase for JSON properties
- Always include pagination for list endpoints
- Version APIs in the URL path (/v1/, /v2/)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create skills 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Create SKILL.md files in .claude/skills/ to give Claude domain knowledge and reusable workflows. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: fix-issue
description: Fix a GitHub issue
disable-model-invocation: true
---
Analyze and fix the GitHub issue: $ARGUMENTS.

1. Use `gh issue view` to get the issue details
2. Understand the problem described in the issue
3. Search the codebase for relevant files
4. Implement the necessary changes to fix the issue
5. Write and run tests to verify the fix
6. Ensure code passes linting and type checking
7. Create a descriptive commit message
8. Push and create a PR

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create custom subagents
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Define specialized assistants in .claude/agents/ that Claude can delegate to for isolated tasks. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: security-reviewer
description: Reviews code for security vulnerabilities
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
model: opus
---
You are a senior security engineer. Review code for:
- Injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS, command injection)
- Authentication and authorization flaws
- Secrets or credentials in code
- Insecure data handling

Provide specific line references and suggested fixes.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Let Claude interview you
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：For larger features, have Claude interview you first. Start with a minimal prompt and ask Claude to interview you using the AskUserQuestion tool. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
I want to build [brief description]. Interview me in detail using the AskUserQuestion tool.

Ask about technical implementation, UI/UX, edge cases, concerns, and tradeoffs. Don't ask obvious questions, dig into the hard parts I might not have considered.

Keep interviewing until we've covered everything, then write a complete spec to SPEC.md.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Use subagents for investigation 1
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Delegate research with "use subagents to investigate X". They explore in a separate context, keeping your main conversation clean for implementation. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Use subagents to investigate how our authentication system handles token
refresh, and whether we have any existing OAuth utilities I should reuse.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Resume conversations
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Run claude --continue to pick up where you left off, or --resume to choose from recent sessions. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
claude --continue    # Resume the most recent conversation
claude --resume      # Select from recent conversations

steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Loop through tasks calling claude -p for each. Use --allowedTools to scope permissions for batch operations. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
for file in $(cat files.txt); do
  claude -p "Migrate $file from React to Vue. Return OK or FAIL." \
    --allowedTools "Edit,Bash(git commit *)"
done

remote-control.sourceLabel: Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲远程控制，也就是人离开电脑了，还能继续盯着本机里的 Claude Code 干活。
手机、平板、浏览器都能接上来看。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你人去镇上了，但院子里的帮工还在干活，你拿手机就能继续喊两句。
这一页会讲要满足哪些前提、怎么在本机开远程会话、怎么从别的设备接进来，以及安全边界。
如果你常常任务做到一半要离开电脑，这页很有用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先在自己最熟的机器上开一次远程会话，确认接入链路跑通。
远程适合续命和盯进度，不适合第一次就在外面做高风险大改。
安全和连接限制要先看清，别只顾方便。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲网页版 Claude Code，也就是把任务放到云上跑，不必全靠你本地机器撑着。
适合异步长任务、跨设备续上工作。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把活送去一个安全工棚里慢慢做，你人不一定守在旁边。
这一页会讲谁能用、怎么开始、网页版到底怎么运作、怎么在网页和终端之间来回接力。
如果你想把长任务丢出去跑，或者在外头也能接着看结果，这页特别值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先拿一个不太重但也不是太小的任务试试网页版节奏。
网页版和终端不是二选一，很多时候是配合着用。
如果任务和本地环境强绑定，还是先想清楚该不该搬到网页上。

#!/bin/bash

# Only run in remote environments
if [ "$CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE" != "true" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

npm install
pip install -r requirements.txt
exit 0

Use setup scripts to install dependencies, configure tools, or prepare anything the cloud environment needs that isn’t in the default image. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Dependency management 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Custom environment images and snapshots are not yet supported. Use setup scripts to install packages when a session starts, or SessionStart hooks for dependency installation that should also run in local environments. SessionStart hooks have known limitations. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Dependency management 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Custom environment images and snapshots are not yet supported. Use setup scripts to install packages when a session starts, or SessionStart hooks for dependency installation that should also run in local environments. SessionStart hooks have known limitations. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Dependency management 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Custom environment images and snapshots are not yet supported. Use setup scripts to install packages when a session starts, or SessionStart hooks for dependency installation that should also run in local environments. steps.code:
#!/bin/bash

# Only run in remote environments
if [ "$CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE" != "true" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

npm install
pip install -r requirements.txt
exit 0

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Chrome 扩展，让 Claude Code 能直接碰浏览器现场。
调网页、看控制台、自动点表单、抓页面数据，这类活都更顺。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你不再只让帮工看图纸，而是直接带它去现场看墙有没有裂、门能不能开。
这一页会讲扩展能干什么、要准备什么、怎么从 CLI 启起来、怎么给站点权限。
如果你做前端、做网页排错，这页很实用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从一个简单页面试，比如本地开发页或测试环境。
浏览器权限别图省事全开，按站点和需要一点点给。
适合网页现场问题，不适合拿来替代所有普通代码分析。

vs-code.sourceLabel: Use Claude Code in VS Code

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 VS Code 集成，把 Claude Code 直接请进你平时写代码的工位。
看 diff、提问、@ 文件、续上会话，都能在编辑器里干。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把帮工请到你手边，不用你在编辑器和终端之间来回跑。
这一页会讲扩展怎么装、怎么开始、提示框怎么用、怎么引用文件和目录、怎么接回以前的会话。
如果你本来就长期待在 VS Code，这页大概率是你的主入口。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先把扩展装好，再拿一个小改动试从编辑器里直接派活。
善用文件引用和历史会话续接，不然它还是容易认路慢。
编辑器里适合边看边改，复杂脚本化操作还是终端更直接。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Reference files and folders
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use @-mentions to give Claude context about specific files or folders. When you type @ followed by a file or folder name, Claude reads that content and can answer questions about it or make changes to it. Claude Code supports fuzzy matching, so you can type partial names to find what you need: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
> Explain the logic in @auth (fuzzy matches auth.js, AuthService.ts, etc.)
> What's in @src/components/ (include a trailing slash for folders)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Automate browser tasks with Chrome
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Connect Claude to your Chrome browser to test web apps, debug with console logs, and automate browser workflows without leaving VS Code. This requires the Claude in Chrome extension version 1.0.36 or higher. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: @browser go to localhost:3000 and check the console for errors

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 JetBrains 系列 IDE 集成，适合 IntelliJ、PyCharm、WebStorm 这些用户。
目的和 VS Code 一样，就是别离开主力工位。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把帮工请进你自己那套熟手工作台，不用你临时换桌子。
这一页会讲支持哪些 IDE、有哪些功能、怎么装、从 IDE 里怎么用、从外部终端怎么配合。
如果你本来就是 JetBrains 老用户，这页最能帮你少折腾。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确认插件装好了、IDE 识别正常，再开始真派活。
越是大工程，越能体会在 IDE 里直接协作比来回切换省劲。
从 IDE 和从外部终端接入的差别最好先试一遍。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 Slack 集成，也就是在团队聊天里直接把活交给 Claude Code。
有人在群里报 bug、提需求、催调查时，不必再来回复制粘贴。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像在大队群里喊一声，帮工就顺着聊天内容去接单。
这一页会讲适合哪些用例、要准备什么、Slack 里怎么接起来、接进去后它怎么流转。
如果你们团队很多协作都在 Slack 里发生，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先拿一类固定场景试，比如报障或代码审查请求。
群里说的话也要尽量清楚，不然它接到的还是糊涂单。
适合把群消息变成任务，不代表群里每句话都该让它接。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Platforms and integrations 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么养专门干单项活的小帮工。
一个查问题，一个写测试，一个整理文档，各干各的，脑子不打架。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 子代理就像把一个大工棚拆成几个小工位。不同活放到不同工位上干，现场更清爽。
这一页会讲内置子代理、怎么快速做第一个子代理、怎么配置、怎么用 `/agents` 管。
如果你发现一个会话里什么活都塞，越干越乱，这页就该上了。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最容易切分的活开始，比如 reviewer、test-writer、docs-helper。
子代理描述一定要写清它负责什么，不负责什么。
作用域别乱放，项目级和个人级要分明，不然迟早互相打架。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Quickstart: create your first subagent 1
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagents are defined in Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. You can create them manually or use the /agents command. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.code:
A code improvement agent that scans files and suggests improvements
for readability, performance, and best practices. It should explain
each issue, show the current code, and provide an improved version.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Quickstart: create your first subagent 3
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagents are defined in Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. You can create them manually or use the /agents command. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Use the code-improver agent to suggest improvements in this project

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Choose the subagent scope
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagents are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Store them in different locations depending on scope. When multiple subagents share the same name, the higher-priority location wins. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Write subagent files
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagent files use YAML frontmatter for configuration, followed by the system prompt in Markdown: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality and best practices
tools: Read, Glob, Grep
model: sonnet
---

You are a code reviewer. When invoked, analyze the code and provide
specific, actionable feedback on quality, security, and best practices.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Control subagent capabilities 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: safe-researcher
description: Research agent with restricted capabilities
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
disallowedTools: Write, Edit
---

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Control subagent capabilities 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:

description: Coordinates work across specialized agents
tools: Agent(worker, researcher), Read, Bash
---

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Control subagent capabilities 3
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Define hooks for subagents 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Subagents can define hooks that run during the subagent’s lifecycle. There are two ways to configure hooks: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Review code changes with automatic linting
hooks:
  PreToolUse:
    - matcher: "Bash"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/validate-command.sh $TOOL_INPUT"
  PostToolUse:
    - matcher: "Edit|Write"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/run-linter.sh"
---

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Understand automatic delegation
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude automatically delegates tasks based on the task description in your request, the description field in subagent configurations, and current context. To encourage proactive delegation, include phrases like “use proactively” in your subagent’s description field. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Use the test-runner subagent to fix failing tests
Have the code-reviewer subagent look at my recent changes

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Common patterns 1
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：One of the most effective uses for subagents is isolating operations that produce large amounts of output. Running tests, fetching documentation, or processing log files can consume significant context. By delegating these to a subagent, the verbose output stays in the subagent’s context while only the relevant summary returns to your main conversation. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Use a subagent to run the test suite and report only the failing tests with their error messages

sections.title: Built-in subagents
paragraphs: 这一节先把系统自带的子代理认清楚：它们各自擅长什么活、会继承什么权限、什么时候会被 Claude 自动叫出来。
看这段时别只盯名字，重点是分清哪类代理偏查资料、哪类能动手改、哪类只适合在计划阶段帮你摸底。

sections.title: Quickstart: create your first subagent
paragraphs: 这一节是手把手做第一个子代理，核心不是炫配置，而是先把入口、存放位置、权限和模型这几步走通。
最稳的顺序就是先进 `/agents`，先做一个最简单、边界很清楚的子代理，跑通以后再往里加复杂能力。

steps.title: Quickstart: create your first subagent 1
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：Subagents are defined in Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. You can create them manually or use the /agents command. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Configure subagents
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Configure subagents”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。

sections.title: Use the /agents command
paragraphs: 这一节讲 `/agents` 这个总入口。以后你想看现有代理、建新代理、改权限、删旧代理，基本都从这里进。
如果不是为了批量自动化，优先用这个交互入口，比你自己手搓文件稳得多。

sections.title: Choose the subagent scope
paragraphs: 这一节讲子代理到底放哪儿。项目级放仓库里，适合团队共用；个人级放 `~/.claude/agents/`，适合你自己到处带着走。
如果只是临时试一下，也可以用 `--agents` 这种会话级方式；但临时的东西不会长期留着，别把正式规则全压在这上面。

steps.title: Choose the subagent scope
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Subagents are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Store them in different locations depending on scope. When multiple subagents share the same name, the higher-priority location wins. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Write subagent files
paragraphs: 这一节讲子代理文件怎么写，重点是 frontmatter 放什么、正文提示词怎么写、改完以后怎么让 Claude 重新认到它。
别把子代理文件写成散文，名字、描述、工具、模型和正文职责要分得清清楚楚。

steps.title: Write subagent files
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Subagent files use YAML frontmatter for configuration, followed by the system prompt in Markdown: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality and best practices
tools: Read, Glob, Grep
model: sonnet
---

You are a code reviewer. When invoked, analyze the code and provide
specific, actionable feedback on quality, security, and best practices.

sections.title: Choose a model
paragraphs: 这一节讲子代理用什么模型。说白了，就是这类活到底该让快一点的工种来，还是让更能深想的工种来。
如果这类子代理主要是查资料、扫目录、跑轻活，通常不用一上来就上最重的模型。

sections.title: Control subagent capabilities
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么收放子代理的手脚，比如它能用哪些工具、能不能再叫别的代理、能不能挂 MCP、skills、memory 这些外接能力。
这里的核心原则就是只给它完成这类活必须的能力，不要为了省事一口气全放开。

steps.title: Control subagent capabilities 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: safe-researcher
description: Research agent with restricted capabilities
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
disallowedTools: Write, Edit
---

steps.title: Control subagent capabilities 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: coordinator
description: Coordinates work across specialized agents
tools: Agent(worker, researcher), Read, Bash
---

steps.title: Control subagent capabilities 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: browser-tester
description: Tests features in a real browser using Playwright
mcpServers:
  # Inline definition: scoped to this subagent only
  - playwright:
      type: stdio
      command: npx
      args: ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@latest"]
  # Reference by name: reuses an already-configured server
  - github
---

Use the Playwright tools to navigate, screenshot, and interact with pages.

steps.title: Control subagent capabilities 4
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：You can control what subagents can do through tool access, permission modes, and conditional rules. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: api-developer
description: Implement API endpoints following team conventions
skills:
  - api-conventions
  - error-handling-patterns
---

Implement API endpoints. Follow the conventions and patterns from the preloaded skills.

sections.title: Define hooks for subagents
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么给子代理挂 hooks，也就是让它在某些动作前后自动做检查、校验或收尾。
如果你想让子代理改文件后自动跑检查，或者在执行危险命令前先过一遍门禁，这一节就是干这个的。

steps.title: Define hooks for subagents 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Subagents can define hooks that run during the subagent’s lifecycle. There are two ways to configure hooks: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Review code changes with automatic linting
hooks:
  PreToolUse:
    - matcher: "Bash"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/validate-command.sh $TOOL_INPUT"
  PostToolUse:
    - matcher: "Edit|Write"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/run-linter.sh"
---

steps.title: Define hooks for subagents 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Subagents can define hooks that run during the subagent’s lifecycle. There are two ways to configure hooks: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Work with subagents
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Work with subagents”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。

sections.title: Understand automatic delegation
paragraphs: 这一节讲 Claude 什么时候会主动把活派给某个子代理。关键不只在你的提问，还在子代理描述写得够不够清楚。
如果你希望某类子代理“该出手时就出手”，描述里就得把触发场景写明白，别只写一串空话。

steps.title: Understand automatic delegation
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：Claude automatically delegates tasks based on the task description in your request, the description field in subagent configurations, and current context. To encourage proactive delegation, include phrases like “use proactively” in your subagent’s description field. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Use the test-runner subagent to fix failing tests
Have the code-reviewer subagent look at my recent changes

sections.title: Run subagents in foreground or background
paragraphs: 这一节讲子代理是在前台拦着你干，还是放到后台并行跑。前台适合需要你随时确认的活，后台适合你想边等边做别的。
后台模式要特别注意提前把权限准备好，不然它中途卡住了，你还得回来补票。

sections.title: Common patterns
paragraphs: 这一节给的是日常最值钱的套路，不是新概念。像把大输出隔离出去、并行调研、串起来分步干，都是子代理最常见的用法。
你看这段时，重点不是全背，而是挑一个最像你当前活路的套路先照着学。

steps.title: Common patterns 1
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：One of the most effective uses for subagents is isolating operations that produce large amounts of output. Running tests, fetching documentation, or processing log files can consume significant context. By delegating these to a subagent, the verbose output stays in the subagent’s context while only the relevant summary returns to your main conversation. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Use a subagent to run the test suite and report only the failing tests with their error messages

steps.title: Common patterns 2
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：One of the most effective uses for subagents is isolating operations that produce large amounts of output. Running tests, fetching documentation, or processing log files can consume significant context. By delegating these to a subagent, the verbose output stays in the subagent’s context while only the relevant summary returns to your main conversation. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Research the authentication, database, and API modules in parallel using separate subagents

steps.title: Common patterns 3
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：One of the most effective uses for subagents is isolating operations that produce large amounts of output. Running tests, fetching documentation, or processing log files can consume significant context. By delegating these to a subagent, the verbose output stays in the subagent’s context while only the relevant summary returns to your main conversation. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Use the code-reviewer subagent to find performance issues, then use the optimizer subagent to fix them

sections.title: Choose between subagents and main conversation
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Choose between subagents and main conversation”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。
看这段时要特别盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。
如果你打算把外接能力往里挂，这里提到的 hooks、MCP、skills、memory 都要分清各自负责哪一摊。

sections.title: Manage subagent context
paragraphs: 这一节讲子代理的上下文怎么续上。每次新起一个子代理都是新会话，但你也可以让 Claude 继续之前那个已经干到一半的子代理。
如果某个子代理已经把现场摸熟了，优先续上它，比重新开一个新代理更省上下文。

steps.title: Manage subagent context 1
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：Each subagent invocation creates a new instance with fresh context. To continue an existing subagent’s work instead of starting over, ask Claude to resume it. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Use the code-reviewer subagent to review the authentication module
[Agent completes]

Continue that code review and now analyze the authorization logic
[Claude resumes the subagent with full context from previous conversation]

steps.title: Manage subagent context 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Each subagent invocation creates a new instance with fresh context. To continue an existing subagent’s work instead of starting over, ask Claude to resume it. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Example subagents
paragraphs: 这一节开始给你看现成样板，不是让你照抄所有细节，而是让你学会怎样把“职责、工具、提示词”三件事写得清楚。
后面每个示例都可以当模板，但真正落你自己项目时，还是要按你们的活路改。

sections.title: Code reviewer
paragraphs: 这一节是代码审查型子代理的样板。重点在于只给它看和查的能力，不让它顺手就改，先把“找问题”这件事做好。
如果你想让审查更稳定，就把输出格式、重点风险和禁止事项写进提示词里。

steps.title: Code reviewer
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A read-only subagent that reviews code without modifying it. This example shows how to design a focused subagent with limited tool access (no Edit or Write) and a detailed prompt that specifies exactly what to look for and how to format output. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Expert code review specialist. Proactively reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability. Use immediately after writing or modifying code.
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
model: inherit
---

You are a senior code reviewer ensuring high standards of code quality and security.

When invoked:
1. Run git diff to see recent changes
2. Focus on modified files
3. Begin review immediately

Review checklist:
- Code is clear and readable
- Functions and variables are well-named
- No duplicated code
- Proper error handling
- No exposed secrets or API keys
- Input validation implemented
- Good test coverage
- Performance considerations addressed

Provide feedback organized by priority:
- Critical issues (must fix)
- Warnings (should fix)
- Suggestions (consider improving)

Include specific examples of how to fix issues.

sections.title: Debugger
paragraphs: 这一节是排错型子代理的样板，适合专门盯报错、失败测试和根因定位这类活。
排错代理最怕信息不全，所以描述里最好写明它该先看什么、怎么判断根因、最后要回给你什么。

steps.title: Debugger
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that can both analyze and fix issues. Unlike the code reviewer, this one includes Edit because fixing bugs requires modifying code. The prompt provides a clear workflow from diagnosis to verification. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: debugger
description: Debugging specialist for errors, test failures, and unexpected behavior. Use proactively when encountering any issues.
tools: Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob
---

You are an expert debugger specializing in root cause analysis.

When invoked:
1. Capture error message and stack trace
2. Identify reproduction steps
3. Isolate the failure location
4. Implement minimal fix
5. Verify solution works

Debugging process:
- Analyze error messages and logs
- Check recent code changes
- Form and test hypotheses
- Add strategic debug logging
- Inspect variable states

For each issue, provide:
- Root cause explanation
- Evidence supporting the diagnosis
- Specific code fix
- Testing approach
- Prevention recommendations

Focus on fixing the underlying issue, not the symptoms.

sections.title: Data scientist
paragraphs: 这一节是数据分析类子代理的样板，适合把查数、做图、解释结果这种活拆给专门角色。
这类代理通常要更强调数据来源、分析边界和输出格式，不然很容易只给你一堆空结论。

steps.title: Data scientist
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A domain-specific subagent for data analysis work. This example shows how to create subagents for specialized workflows outside of typical coding tasks. It explicitly sets model: sonnet for more capable analysis. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: data-scientist
description: Data analysis expert for SQL queries, BigQuery operations, and data insights. Use proactively for data analysis tasks and queries.
tools: Bash, Read, Write
model: sonnet
---

You are a data scientist specializing in SQL and BigQuery analysis.

When invoked:
1. Understand the data analysis requirement
2. Write efficient SQL queries
3. Use BigQuery command line tools (bq) when appropriate
4. Analyze and summarize results
5. Present findings clearly

Key practices:
- Write optimized SQL queries with proper filters
- Use appropriate aggregations and joins
- Include comments explaining complex logic
- Format results for readability
- Provide data-driven recommendations

For each analysis:
- Explain the query approach
- Document any assumptions
- Highlight key findings
- Suggest next steps based on data

Always ensure queries are efficient and cost-effective.

sections.title: Database query validator
paragraphs: 这一节讲一个很实用的样板：数据库查询代理可以放开 Bash，但要先挂校验，只允许读，不允许改。
重点不是“会查库”本身，而是先把危险动作挡在门外，确保它问库时只读不写。

steps.title: Database query validator 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: db-reader
description: Execute read-only database queries. Use when analyzing data or generating reports.
tools: Bash
hooks:
  PreToolUse:
    - matcher: "Bash"
      hooks:
        - type: command
          command: "./scripts/validate-readonly-query.sh"
---

You are a database analyst with read-only access. Execute SELECT queries to answer questions about the data.

When asked to analyze data:
1. Identify which tables contain the relevant data
2. Write efficient SELECT queries with appropriate filters
3. Present results clearly with context

You cannot modify data. If asked to INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or modify schema, explain that you only have read access.

steps.title: Database query validator 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Blocks SQL write operations, allows SELECT queries

# Read JSON input from stdin
INPUT=$(cat)

# Extract the command field from tool_input using jq
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty')

if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

# Block write operations (case-insensitive)
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -iE '\b(INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE|DROP|CREATE|ALTER|TRUNCATE|REPLACE|MERGE)\b' > /dev/null; then
  echo "Blocked: Write operations not allowed. Use SELECT queries only." >&2
  exit 2
fi

exit 0

steps.title: Database query validator 3
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：A subagent that allows Bash access but validates commands to permit only read-only SQL queries. This example shows how to use PreToolUse hooks for conditional validation when you need finer control than the tools field provides. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Next steps
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Next steps”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。
这里还牵扯作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。
看这段时要特别盯工具和权限边界，别为了省事一把全开。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

agent-teams.sourceLabel: Orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲代理团队，也就是让多名 Claude 同时协作。
适合任务大、分工明确、需要并行推进的场景。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这比子代理更像真施工队，不是一个人换帽子，而是几个人同时上工。
这一页会讲什么时候该用团队、它和子代理有啥区别、怎么开团队、怎么控制队员。
如果你的任务已经大到“一个人来回切角色很累”，团队模式就有价值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确认任务真能拆开，再拉团队，不然只会多出协调成本。
最稳的分法一般是勘察、实现、验收三类人分开。
一开始团队别拉太大，先两三名角色试顺，再扩大。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Enable agent teams
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Agent teams are disabled by default. Enable them by setting the CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS environment variable to 1, either in your shell environment or through settings.json: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Start your first agent team
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：After enabling agent teams, tell Claude to create an agent team and describe the task and the team structure you want in natural language. Claude creates the team, spawns teammates, and coordinates work based on your prompt. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
I'm designing a CLI tool that helps developers track TODO comments across
their codebase. Create an agent team to explore this from different angles: one
teammate on UX, one on technical architecture, one playing devil's advocate.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Choose a display mode 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Agent teams support two display modes: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Choose a display mode 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Agent teams support two display modes: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Specify teammates and models
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude decides the number of teammates to spawn based on your task, or you can specify exactly what you want: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Create a team with 4 teammates to refactor these modules in parallel.
Use Sonnet for each teammate.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Require plan approval for teammates
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：For complex or risky tasks, you can require teammates to plan before implementing. The teammate works in read-only plan mode until the lead approves their approach: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Spawn an architect teammate to refactor the authentication module.
Require plan approval before they make any changes.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Shut down teammates
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：To gracefully end a teammate’s session: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Ask the researcher teammate to shut down

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Clean up the team
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：When you’re done, ask the lead to clean up: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Run a parallel code review
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：A single reviewer tends to gravitate toward one type of issue at a time. Splitting review criteria into independent domains means security, performance, and test coverage all get thorough attention simultaneously. The prompt assigns each teammate a distinct lens so they don’t overlap: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Create an agent team to review PR #142. Spawn three reviewers:
- One focused on security implications
- One checking performance impact
- One validating test coverage
Have them each review and report findings.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Investigate with competing hypotheses
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：When the root cause is unclear, a single agent tends to find one plausible explanation and stop looking. The prompt fights this by making teammates explicitly adversarial: each one’s job is not only to investigate its own theory but to challenge the others’. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Users report the app exits after one message instead of staying connected.
Spawn 5 agent teammates to investigate different hypotheses. Have them talk to
each other to try to disprove each other's theories, like a scientific
debate. Update the findings doc with whatever consensus emerges.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Give teammates enough context
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Teammates load project context automatically, including CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills, but they don’t inherit the lead’s conversation history. See Context and communication for details. Include task-specific details in the spawn prompt: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Spawn a security reviewer teammate with the prompt: "Review the authentication module
at src/auth/ for security vulnerabilities. Focus on token handling, session
management, and input validation. The app uses JWT tokens stored in
httpOnly cookies. Report any issues with severity ratings."

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Wait for teammates to finish
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：Sometimes the lead starts implementing tasks itself instead of waiting for teammates. If you notice this: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Wait for your teammates to complete their tasks before proceeding

sections.title: When to use agent teams
paragraphs: 这一节先讲什么时候值得开团队。只有任务能明显拆成几股并行推进时，团队模式才值；顺着一条线一步步做的活，单会话反而更省。
团队最大的好处是并行摸底、并行验证、分层协作；最大的代价是协调成本和 token 开销都更高。

sections.title: Compare with subagents
paragraphs: 这一节是拿 agent teams 和 subagents 做对比。简单说，子代理更像几个短工各自回报；团队更像几个人边干边彼此沟通。
如果你的工人之间需要互相交换发现、挑战彼此方案，再决定怎么收口，那就更偏团队。

sections.title: Enable agent teams
paragraphs: 这一节讲团队模式默认是关着的，要先把实验开关打开，Claude 才能真的拉起一个队伍来干活。
最常见的做法就是在环境变量或 `settings.json` 里把对应开关设成 `1`，先开起来再试。

steps.title: Enable agent teams
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Agent teams are disabled by default. Enable them by setting the CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS environment variable to 1, either in your shell environment or through settings.json: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Start your first agent team
paragraphs: 这一节是第一次拉团队开工。做法不是先手点很多按钮，而是直接用自然语言告诉 Claude：这活要分成哪几路、每一路干什么。
样例里强调的不是文案本身，而是“角色之间要能并行、不互相卡脖子”这个原则。

steps.title: Start your first agent team
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：After enabling agent teams, tell Claude to create an agent team and describe the task and the team structure you want in natural language. Claude creates the team, spawns teammates, and coordinates work based on your prompt. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
I'm designing a CLI tool that helps developers track TODO comments across
their codebase. Create an agent team to explore this from different angles: one
teammate on UX, one on technical architecture, one playing devil's advocate.

sections.title: Control your agent team
paragraphs: 这一节讲团队拉起来以后怎么指挥。你平时主要还是跟 lead 说话，由它来拆任务、派活、协调收口。
别把每个细碎指令都自己塞给所有队友，先让 lead 做总调度，团队才不容易乱。

sections.title: Choose a display mode
paragraphs: 这一节讲团队输出怎么显示。一个模式是在主终端里轮流切着看，另一个模式是分屏，一人一格同时盯着。
如果你想同时看多个人在干什么，就走分屏；如果环境简单、先求能跑，就先用 in-process。

steps.title: Choose a display mode 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Agent teams support two display modes: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Choose a display mode 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Agent teams support two display modes: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Specify teammates and models
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么明确告诉 Claude：你要几名队友、各自大概干什么、用什么模型。
如果你不说，Claude 会自己估；但任务一旦比较重，提前把人数和模型说死，通常更稳。

steps.title: Specify teammates and models
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：Claude decides the number of teammates to spawn based on your task, or you can specify exactly what you want: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Create a team with 4 teammates to refactor these modules in parallel.
Use Sonnet for each teammate.

sections.title: Require plan approval for teammates
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么让队友先交计划，再动手。对高风险改动，这是很值的一道闸门。
思路很简单：先只读计划，lead 审过了再放它进实施，不满意就打回去重做。

steps.title: Require plan approval for teammates
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：For complex or risky tasks, you can require teammates to plan before implementing. The teammate works in read-only plan mode until the lead approves their approach: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Spawn an architect teammate to refactor the authentication module.
Require plan approval before they make any changes.

sections.title: Talk to teammates directly
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Talk to teammates directly”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。
这里还牵扯作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。
这一段还会谈显示方式和终端环境，重点是先选一个你当前最稳能用的模式，不必一上来追求花哨。

sections.title: Assign and claim tasks
paragraphs: 这一节讲团队任务单怎么分。可以由 lead 指派，也可以让队友干完手头活后自己认领下一个没被卡住的任务。
你要盯的是依赖关系和占坑冲突，别让两个队友同时冲向同一块地。

sections.title: Shut down teammates
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么优雅地让某个队友收工。别直接粗暴掐掉，最好通过 lead 发关机请求，让对方把正在做的活收好。
这样收尾更干净，也不容易把共享任务单和上下文搞坏。

steps.title: Shut down teammates
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：To gracefully end a teammate’s session: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: Ask the researcher teammate to shut down

sections.title: Clean up the team
paragraphs: 这一节讲团队收尾，不是随便喊一句完事，而是要让 lead 负责做 cleanup，把共享资源收干净。
最关键的规矩是：先把还在跑的队友停掉，再让 lead 做 cleanup；不要让普通 teammate 自己去收尾。

steps.title: Clean up the team
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一节原文主要在讲：When you’re done, ask the lead to clean up: 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

sections.title: Enforce quality gates with hooks
paragraphs: 这一节讲怎么在团队流程里加质检门。队友快空闲时、任务准备标完成时，都可以用 hooks 再拦一道。
如果检查没过，可以用退出码把任务打回去，让队友继续干，而不是稀里糊涂就算完成。

steps.title: Run a parallel code review
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：A single reviewer tends to gravitate toward one type of issue at a time. Splitting review criteria into independent domains means security, performance, and test coverage all get thorough attention simultaneously. The prompt assigns each teammate a distinct lens so they don’t overlap: 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Create an agent team to review PR #142. Spawn three reviewers:
- One focused on security implications
- One checking performance impact
- One validating test coverage
Have them each review and report findings.

sections.title: Investigate with competing hypotheses
paragraphs: 这一节主要在讲“Investigate with competing hypotheses”这块该怎么理解、什么时候用，以及要盯住哪些边界。

steps.title: Investigate with competing hypotheses
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：When the root cause is unclear, a single agent tends to find one plausible explanation and stop looking. The prompt fights this by making teammates explicitly adversarial: each one’s job is not only to investigate its own theory but to challenge the others’. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
Users report the app exits after one message instead of staying connected.
Spawn 5 agent teammates to investigate different hypotheses. Have them talk to
each other to try to disprove each other's theories, like a scientific
debate. Update the findings doc with whatever consensus emerges.

Spawn a security reviewer teammate with the prompt: "Review the authentication module
at src/auth/ for security vulnerabilities. Focus on token handling, session
management, and input validation. The app uses JWT tokens stored in
httpOnly cookies. Report any issues with severity ratings."

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么做自己的插件，把技能、代理、hooks、MCP 这些打成包。
做出来以后，别的项目和别人装上就能直接用。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 插件像打包好的工具箱，不用每换一块地就重新配一遍。
这一页会讲什么时候该做插件、什么时候只做独立配置就够、以及第一个插件怎么起步。
如果你们团队总在重复配同一套东西，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先确定你打包的是一类稳定能力，而不是一堆临时想法。
从最小插件结构做起，先让它能装、能跑，再慢慢加复杂内容。
技能、hooks、代理、MCP 往里放时，先想好谁是核心，不要把插件做成杂货铺。

discover-plugins.sourceLabel: Discover and install prebuilt plugins through marketplaces

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲去哪找现成插件、怎么装、装完能多出哪些本事。
重点不是装得多，而是装得准。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像去农具市场挑现成家伙。别人已经打磨好的，拿来就能省你很多重复造轮子的劲。
这一页还会讲市场怎么运作、官方市场里有什么、代码智能插件和外部集成插件能带来什么。
如果你懒得自己从零做插件，这页就是最省力的入口。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先装最贴近你当前活路的插件，别为了新鲜一口气装一堆。
看插件时，除了功能，也要看它到底会给 Claude 多开哪些能力。
装完先在小项目试，再决定要不要推广到主项目和团队里。

steps.title: Configure auto-updates
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：Claude Code can automatically update marketplaces and their installed plugins at startup. When auto-update is enabled for a marketplace, Claude Code refreshes the marketplace data and updates installed plugins to their latest versions. If any plugins were updated, you’ll see a notification prompting you to run /reload-plugins. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

这里还牵扯作用域，意思就是这条规则到底管当前项目、你个人，还是只管这一趟会话。
如果你看到环境变量或 settings.json，意思通常都是：这不是会话里临时喊一声就行，而是要把开关真正写进环境或配置。

steps.title: Configure team marketplaces
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Team admins can set up automatic marketplace installation for projects by adding marketplace configuration to .claude/settings.json. When team members trust the repository folder, Claude Code prompts them to install these marketplaces and plugins. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

illustration.title: 一眼看懂这一页
lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲技能，也就是把常用绝活做成随叫随到的小抄。
适合把团队里重复用的套路固化下来。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 技能像写在墙上的拿手活说明：遇到这种事，就按这个打法来。
这一页会讲内置技能、怎么做第一个技能、技能放哪儿、怎么自动发现。
如果你老是在反复教 Claude 同一种套路，这页就是答案。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 技能要短、准、能执行，不要写成一篇空洞演讲稿。
先从一个高频场景试，比如写测试、审查代码、生成文档。
目录结构和发现规则先弄清，不然写了半天找不着。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: explain-code
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---

When explaining code, always include:

1. **Start with an analogy**: Compare the code to something from everyday life
2. **Draw a diagram**: Use ASCII art to show the flow, structure, or relationships
3. **Walk through the code**: Explain step-by-step what happens
4. **Highlight a gotcha**: What's a common mistake or misconception?

Keep explanations conversational. For complex concepts, use multiple analogies.

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 3
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create your first skill 4
steps.body: 这一步是你直接跟 Claude 说的话。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example creates a skill that teaches Claude to explain code using visual diagrams and analogies. Since it uses default frontmatter, Claude can load it automatically when you ask how something works, or you can invoke it directly with /explain-code. 下面这块属于“提问示例”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Where skills live
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Where you store a skill determines who can use it: 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md           # Main instructions (required)
├── template.md        # Template for Claude to fill in
├── examples/
│   └── sample.md      # Example output showing expected format
└── scripts/
    └── validate.sh    # Script Claude can execute

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Types of skill content 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skill files can contain any instructions, but thinking about how you want to invoke them helps guide what to include: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: api-conventions
description: API design patterns for this codebase
---

When writing API endpoints:
- Use RESTful naming conventions
- Return consistent error formats
- Include request validation

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Types of skill content 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skill files can contain any instructions, but thinking about how you want to invoke them helps guide what to include: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
context: fork
disable-model-invocation: true
---

Deploy the application:
1. Run the test suite
2. Build the application
3. Push to the deployment target

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Frontmatter reference 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Beyond the markdown content, you can configure skill behavior using YAML frontmatter fields between --- markers at the top of your SKILL.md file: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: my-skill
description: What this skill does
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Read, Grep
---

Your skill instructions here...

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Frontmatter reference 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Beyond the markdown content, you can configure skill behavior using YAML frontmatter fields between --- markers at the top of your SKILL.md file: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: session-logger
description: Log activity for this session
---

Log the following to logs/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}.log:

$ARGUMENTS

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Add supporting files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你看目录和文件该怎么摆。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skills can include multiple files in their directory. This keeps SKILL.md focused on the essentials while letting Claude access detailed reference material only when needed. Large reference docs, API specifications, or example collections don’t need to load into context every time the skill runs. 下面这块属于“目录结构”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md (required - overview and navigation)
├── reference.md (detailed API docs - loaded when needed)
├── examples.md (usage examples - loaded when needed)
└── scripts/
    └── helper.py (utility script - executed, not loaded)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Add supporting files 2
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一小节原文主要在讲：Skills can include multiple files in their directory. This keeps SKILL.md focused on the essentials while letting Claude access detailed reference material only when needed. Large reference docs, API specifications, or example collections don’t need to load into context every time the skill runs. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
## Additional resources

- For complete API details, see [reference.md](reference.md)
- For usage examples, see [examples.md](examples.md)

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Control who invokes a skill
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：By default, both you and Claude can invoke any skill. You can type /skill-name to invoke it directly, and Claude can load it automatically when relevant to your conversation. Two frontmatter fields let you restrict this: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
---

Deploy $ARGUMENTS to production:

1. Run the test suite
2. Build the application
3. Push to the deployment target
4. Verify the deployment succeeded

steps.title: Inject dynamic context
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：The !command“ syntax runs shell commands before the skill content is sent to Claude. The command output replaces the placeholder, so Claude receives actual data, not the command itself. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: pr-summary
description: Summarize changes in a pull request
context: fork
agent: Explore
allowed-tools: Bash(gh *)
---

## Pull request context
- PR diff: !`gh pr diff`
- PR comments: !`gh pr view --comments`
- Changed files: !`gh pr diff --name-only`

## Your task
Summarize this pull request...

steps.title: Restrict Claude’s skill access 1
steps.body: 这一步是原页里的关键代码或示例。这一节原文主要在讲：By default, Claude can invoke any skill that doesn’t have disable-model-invocation: true set. Skills that define allowed-tools grant Claude access to those tools without per-use approval when the skill is active. Your permission settings still govern baseline approval behavior for all other tools. Built-in commands like /compact and /init are not available through the Skill tool. 下面这块属于“代码片段”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

One powerful pattern is generating visual output: interactive HTML files that open in your browser for exploring data, debugging, or creating reports. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
---
name: codebase-visualizer
description: Generate an interactive collapsible tree visualization of your codebase. Use when exploring a new repo, understanding project structure, or identifying large files.
allowed-tools: Bash(python *)
---

# Codebase Visualizer

Generate an interactive HTML tree view that shows your project's file structure with collapsible directories.

## Usage

Run the visualization script from your project root:

```bash
python ~/.claude/skills/codebase-visualizer/scripts/visualize.py .
```text

This creates `codebase-map.html` in the current directory and opens it in your default browser.

## What the visualization shows

- **Collapsible directories**: Click folders to expand/collapse
- **File sizes**: Displayed next to each file
- **Colors**: Different colors for different file types
- **Directory totals**: Shows aggregate size of each folder

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲定时任务，让 Claude 到点自动跑提示。
适合循环提醒、轮询状态、固定时间做一遍检查。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像你让帮工每天早上先去看一眼水泵，每周一再盘一次仓库。
这一页会讲 `/loop` 怎么用、间隔语法、一次性提醒、任务怎么管理。
如果你总有重复催一次的事，这页特别省心。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最简单的重复活开始，不要一开始就排一大串复杂任务。
循环间隔和到期逻辑要看清，别设完就不管。
任务真跑起来后，要定期回头看有没有空转或误报。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲输出风格，意思是 Claude 最后把结果说成什么样。
不只是写代码时能用，做摘要、报告、清单、结构化输出时也很有用。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把它想成给帮工提前定口径。是让它写成清单、写成报告、写成像客服话术，还是写成结构化格式，先说好，后面省很多返工。
这一页特别实用，因为很多时候问题不在 Claude 不会，而在它说出来的样子不合用。
如果你想把 Claude 从“程序员帮手”扩到更多工作场景，这页很值。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先用内置输出风格试手，确认哪种最适合你的活。
真要自定义时，先把格式目标讲清楚，不要又想自由发挥又想字段固定。
这页最好和 `CLAUDE.md`、agents、append-system-prompt 一起分清楚谁管什么。

改完文件自动格式化、任务结束发提醒、碰到危险命令先拦一下，都属于这类。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: Hooks 像门口的检查员和工地边上的规矩牌。事情发生到某一步，就自动触发。
这一页会讲第一个 hook 怎么配、能自动化什么、怎么提醒输入、怎么改完就自动格式化。
如果你想把规矩落地，不想每次靠人记，这页很好用。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最稳的 hook 开始，比如格式化、通知、阻止改受保护文件。
触发时机一定要分清，不然 hook 一多就容易互相绊。
写 hook 时别贪重，越复杂越难维护。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Block edits to protected files 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Prevent Claude from modifying sensitive files like .env, package-lock.json, or anything in .git/. Claude receives feedback explaining why the edit was blocked, so it can adjust its approach. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
# protect-files.sh

INPUT=$(cat)
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')

PROTECTED_PATTERNS=(".env" "package-lock.json" ".git/")

for pattern in "${PROTECTED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"$pattern"* ]]; then
    echo "Blocked: $FILE_PATH matches protected pattern '$pattern'" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
done

exit 0

steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Prevent Claude from modifying sensitive files like .env, package-lock.json, or anything in .git/. Claude receives feedback explaining why the edit was blocked, so it can adjust its approach. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Read input and return output 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Hooks communicate with Claude Code through stdin, stdout, stderr, and exit codes. When an event fires, Claude Code passes event-specific data as JSON to your script’s stdin. Your script reads that data, does its work, and tells Claude Code what to do next via the exit code. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Read input and return output 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Hooks communicate with Claude Code through stdin, stdout, stderr, and exit codes. When an event fires, Claude Code passes event-specific data as JSON to your script’s stdin. Your script reads that data, does its work, and tells Claude Code what to do next via the exit code. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -q "drop table"; then
  echo "Blocked: dropping tables is not allowed" >&2  # stderr becomes Claude's feedback
  exit 2                                               # exit 2 = block the action
fi

exit 0  # exit 0 = let it proceed

steps.title: Read input and return output 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Hooks communicate with Claude Code through stdin, stdout, stderr, and exit codes. Your script reads that data, does its work, and tells Claude Code what to do next via the exit code. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Filter hooks with matchers 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：Without a matcher, a hook fires on every occurrence of its event. Matchers let you narrow that down. For example, if you want to run a formatter only after file edits (not after every tool call), add a matcher to your PostToolUse hook: 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Prompt-based hooks
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一节原文主要在讲：For decisions that require judgment rather than deterministic rules, use type: "prompt" hooks. Instead of running a shell command, Claude Code sends your prompt and the hook’s input data to a Claude model (Haiku by default) to make the decision. You can specify a different model with the model field if you need more capability. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: Agent-based hooks

Unlike prompt hooks which make a single LLM call, agent hooks spawn a subagent that can read files, search code, and use other tools to verify conditions before returning a decision. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一节原文主要在讲：Claude keeps working in an infinite loop instead of stopping. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(cat)
if [ "$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active')" = "true" ]; then
  exit 0  # Allow Claude to stop
fi
# ... rest of your hook logic

lines: 这是 Build with Claude Code 里的一摊要紧活
illustration.caption: 先把这页到底在讲什么看明白，再去碰具体命令和配置，最不容易绕晕。

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲怎么把 Claude Code 编程化调用，不用每次都手工进对话界面。
适合脚本、命令行流程、Python、TypeScript 这些自动化场景。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 这像把帮工从院子里请到流水线上。你不是站在旁边一句句吩咐，而是把活写进程序，让它照着跑。
这页会讲基本调用、结构化输出、流式返回、自动批准工具这些能力。
如果你想把 Claude 接进你自己的程序或 CI，这页非常关键。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 先从最小示例跑通，别一上来就接整条生产流水。
如果后面还要给别的程序继续吃结果，优先研究结构化输出。
自动批准工具和自动提交这类能力要特别慎重，最好先小范围试。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Basic usage
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Add the -p (or --print) flag to any claude command to run it non-interactively. All CLI options work with -p, including: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code: claude -p "What does the auth module do?"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Get structured output 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --output-format to control how responses are returned: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Get structured output 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --output-format to control how responses are returned: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
claude -p "Extract the main function names from auth.py" \
  --output-format json \
  --json-schema '{"type":"object","properties":{"functions":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}}},"required":["functions"]}'

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Get structured output 3
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --output-format to control how responses are returned: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Extract the text result
claude -p "Summarize this project" --output-format json | jq -r '.result'

# Extract structured output
claude -p "Extract function names from auth.py" \
  --output-format json \
  --json-schema '{"type":"object","properties":{"functions":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}}},"required":["functions"]}' \
  | jq '.structured_output'

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Stream responses 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --output-format stream-json with --verbose and --include-partial-messages to receive tokens as they’re generated. Each line is a JSON object representing an event: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Stream responses 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --output-format stream-json with --verbose and --include-partial-messages to receive tokens as they’re generated. Each line is a JSON object representing an event: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
claude -p "Write a poem" --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages | \
  jq -rj 'select(.type == "stream_event" and .event.delta.type? == "text_delta") | .event.delta.text'

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Auto-approve tools
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --allowedTools to let Claude use certain tools without prompting. This example runs a test suite and fixes failures, allowing Claude to execute Bash commands and read/edit files without asking for permission: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
claude -p "Run the test suite and fix any failures" \
  --allowedTools "Bash,Read,Edit"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Create a commit
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：This example reviews staged changes and creates a commit with an appropriate message: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
claude -p "Look at my staged changes and create an appropriate commit" \
  --allowedTools "Bash(git diff *),Bash(git log *),Bash(git status *),Bash(git commit *)"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Customize the system prompt
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --append-system-prompt to add instructions while keeping Claude Code’s default behavior. This example pipes a PR diff to Claude and instructs it to review for security vulnerabilities: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
gh pr diff "$1" | claude -p \
  --append-system-prompt "You are a security engineer. Review for vulnerabilities." \
  --output-format json

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Continue conversations 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --continue to continue the most recent conversation, or --resume with a session ID to continue a specific conversation. This example runs a review, then sends follow-up prompts: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# First request
claude -p "Review this codebase for performance issues"

# Continue the most recent conversation
claude -p "Now focus on the database queries" --continue
claude -p "Generate a summary of all issues found" --continue

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Continue conversations 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Use --continue to continue the most recent conversation, or --resume with a session ID to continue a specific conversation. This example runs a review, then sends follow-up prompts: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
session_id=$(claude -p "Start a review" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id')
claude -p "Continue that review" --resume "$session_id"

mcp.sourceLabel: Connect Claude Code to tools via MCP

sections.title: 简要总结
paragraphs: 这页讲 MCP，也就是怎么把 Claude Code 接到外部工具和数据源上。
说白了，就是给帮工装外接设备，让它别只盯着眼前这堆文件。

sections.title: 农民伯伯版解释
paragraphs: 你可以把 MCP 想成拖拉机后面的挂载口。接上去以后，Claude 才能碰到数据库、接口、知识库、内部工具这些外面的家伙事。
这页最值钱的地方，不是名词本身，而是它会告诉你有哪些接法、哪些现成服务器、该怎么装。
如果你想让 Claude 真正懂你们团队外面的系统，这页几乎绕不过去。

sections.title: 上手时重点盯住什么
paragraphs: 第一次上 MCP，先挑一个最刚需的外部工具接，不要一口气连十几个。
远程 HTTP、SSE、本地 stdio 这些接法分清楚，再决定走哪条。
接好后先做只读类试验，确认它看得懂、用得稳，再考虑更重的操作。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Option 1: Add a remote HTTP server
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：HTTP servers are the recommended option for connecting to remote MCP servers. This is the most widely supported transport for cloud-based services. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Basic syntax
claude mcp add --transport http <name> <url>

# Real example: Connect to Notion
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp

# Example with Bearer token
claude mcp add --transport http secure-api https://api.example.com/mcp \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer your-token"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Option 2: Add a remote SSE server
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：The SSE (Server-Sent Events) transport is deprecated. Use HTTP servers instead, where available. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Basic syntax
claude mcp add --transport sse <name> <url>

# Real example: Connect to Asana
claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse

# Example with authentication header
claude mcp add --transport sse private-api https://api.company.com/sse \
  --header "X-API-Key: your-key-here"

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Option 3: Add a local stdio server
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Stdio servers run as local processes on your machine. They’re ideal for tools that need direct system access or custom scripts. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Basic syntax
claude mcp add [options] <name> -- <command> [args...]

# Real example: Add Airtable server
claude mcp add --transport stdio --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY airtable \
  -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Managing your servers
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Once configured, you can manage your MCP servers with these commands: 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# List all configured servers
claude mcp list

# Get details for a specific server
claude mcp get github

# Remove a server
claude mcp remove github

# (within Claude Code) Check server status
/mcp

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Dynamic tool updates
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Claude Code supports MCP list_changed notifications, allowing MCP servers to dynamically update their available tools, prompts, and resources without requiring you to disconnect and reconnect. When an MCP server sends a list_changed notification, Claude Code automatically refreshes the available capabilities from that server. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# This creates command="cmd" which Windows can execute
claude mcp add --transport stdio my-server -- cmd /c npx -y @some/package

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Plugin-provided MCP servers 1

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Local scope
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Local-scoped servers represent the default configuration level and are stored in ~/.claude.json under your project’s path. These servers remain private to you and are only accessible when working within the current project directory. This scope is ideal for personal development servers, experimental configurations, or servers containing sensitive credentials that shouldn’t be shared. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Add a local-scoped server (default)
claude mcp add --transport http stripe https://mcp.stripe.com

# Explicitly specify local scope
claude mcp add --transport http stripe --scope local https://mcp.stripe.com

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Project scope 1
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：Project-scoped servers enable team collaboration by storing configurations in a .mcp.json file at your project’s root directory. This file is designed to be checked into version control, ensuring all team members have access to the same MCP tools and services. When you add a project-scoped server, Claude Code automatically creates or updates this file with the appropriate configuration structure. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Add a project-scoped server
claude mcp add --transport http paypal --scope project https://mcp.paypal.com/mcp

steps.title: 原页关键片段：Project scope 2
steps.body: 这一步是让你改配置或新建配置文件。这一小节原文主要在讲：Project-scoped servers enable team collaboration by storing configurations in a .mcp.json file at your project’s root directory. This file is designed to be checked into version control, ensuring all team members have access to the same MCP tools and services. When you add a project-scoped server, Claude Code automatically creates or updates this file with the appropriate configuration structure. 下面这块属于“配置”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。

steps.title: 原页关键片段：User scope
steps.body: 这一步是让你在终端里敲命令。这一小节原文主要在讲：User-scoped servers are stored in ~/.claude.json and provide cross-project accessibility, making them available across all projects on your machine while remaining private to your user account. This scope works well for personal utility servers, development tools, or services you frequently use across different projects. 下面这块属于“命令”，关键命令、文件名和参数最好照原样保留。
steps.code:
# Add a user server
claude mcp add --transport http hubspot --scope user https://mcp.hubspot.com/anthropic
